title: A Bad Day
learningGoal: The learner will construct sentences utilizing past tense negative adjectives and express desire to return home.
instructions: Write four Japanese sentences describing a bad trip. You will need to use past tense negative adjectives. Remember, 'Tanoshii' is an i-adjective, so its negative past is 'Tanoshikunakatta'.

contentItems.text: For the desire form 'want to go', conjugate Ikimasu -> Ikitai desu.

writingPrompt: Translate: 1) Yesterday, I went to Tokyo. 2) It was not fun. 3) The movie was boring. 4) I want to go home.
promptGoal: To construct complex past-negative conjugations of i-adjectives and use the ~tai form.

modelExampleOutputs.targetText: きのう、とうきょうへ いきました。たのしくなかったです。えいがは つまらなかったです。うちへ かえりたいです。
modelExampleOutputs.transliteration: Kinou, Toukyou e ikimashita. Tanoshikunakatta desu. Eiga wa tsumaranakatta desu. Uchi e kaeritai desu.
modelExampleOutputs.baseTranslation: Yesterday, I went to Tokyo. It was not fun. The movie was boring. I want to return home.
modelExampleOutputs.annotation: Perfect conjugation of negative past (kunakatta) and positive past (katta) for i-adjectives, and desire form (tai).

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: きのう、とうきょうへ いきました。たのしくなかったです。えいがは つまらなかったです。うちへ かえりたいです。
acceptableAlternates: きのう、とうきょうに いきました。たのしくなかったです。えいがは つまらなかったです。うちへ かえりたいです。
きのう とうきょうへ いきました。それは たのしくなかったです。えいがは つまらなかったです。うちへ かえりたいです。

feedbackRules.onPass: Astounding! You perfectly conjugated these very long and complex Japanese words.
feedbackRules.onFail: Review the past tense of i-adjectives. Drop the 'i' and add 'katta' for positive past. Drop the 'i' and add 'kunakatta' for negative past.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good try! Make sure you didn't write 'Tanoshii ja arimasen deshita'. Tanoshii is an i-adjective, not a na-adjective.

hintText: To make 'Tanoshii' (fun) into 'was not fun', drop the 'i' and add 'kunakatta'.
commonMistakes: Writing 'Tanoshiikunakatta' (forgetting to drop the first i).
